rationale: The citing paper’s stated theory and contributions focus on national identity threats, consumer ethnocentrism/animosity, and mitigation ad messaging, while the cited paper is about logo dynamism/perceived movement and engagement effects in logo design. None of the citing note’s focal constructs or relation-to-prior-work summary indicates reliance on logo-dynamism theory or methods. With only a single DOI-level match and no in-text evidence of substantive use, this looks like an incidental/background mention.
